description: You are an astrophysics student analyzing a Kepler Space Telescope light curve for the star Kepler-8 to measure its exoplanet using data from ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/kepler8b_lightcurve.csv and ~/Documents/system_parameters.txt. Using ONLYOFFICE Spreadsheet, compute the normalized flux (SAP_FLUX divided by the average SAP_FLUX), phase (MOD((TIME - 134.0), 3.5225) / 3.5225), and centered phase (IF(Phase > 0.5, Phase - 1, Phase)) for each row. Calculate summary metrics for 'Transit Depth' (1 - MIN(Normalized Flux)), 'Radius Ratio' (SQRT(Transit Depth)), and 'Planet Radius' (Radius Ratio * 1.48 * 9.73), ensuring each value is accompanied by a clear text label. Save the completed analysis as an Excel workbook to ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/kepler8b_analysis.xlsx.

metadata.data_source: Synthetic realistic model based on Kepler-8b MAST archival data
